skill.name: safe-debug
skill.description: Conservative debugging workflow for deep learning research failures. It helps diagnose tracebacks, CUDA memory errors, checkpoint mismatches, shape issues, NaN losses, and training failures before any code changes.
skill.summary: Diagnoses deep learning research failures before patching.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography and shell execution patterns, but review found these were false positives from prose, Markdown backticks, and Python f-strings. The confirmed concerns are bounded filesystem behavior in the helper script and parent-directory references in the skill instructions. No evidence found of malicious intent, network access, credential access, shell execution, or prompt injection.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled File Read and Report Output Paths
medium_findings.description: The helper script accepts an error file path and an output directory path from command-line arguments. This is legitimate for a debugging helper, but an agent or user could read unintended local text files or write reports outside the workspace if unsafe paths are supplied.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and output directory are directly controlled by CLI arguments. The behavior is visible and utility-focused, but it still creates a filesystem boundary risk.

medium_findings.title: Out-of-Package Reference Paths
medium_findings.description: The skill instructions reference files two directories above the skill package. This can cause an agent to look outside the packaged skill boundary and depend on context that may not be present or reviewed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The parent-directory paths are explicit in the skill instructions. They do not prove malicious intent, but they are a clear packaging and boundary concern.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts in the YAML, Python, and Markdown files are false positives. The cited lines contain descriptions or normal prose, not cryptographic APIs, hashes, ciphers, or encryption logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context contains no cryptography implementation or configuration. The scanner appears to match substrings inside ordinary descriptive text.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Shell Execution Matches
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution alerts are false positives. The cited Python lines use formatted strings and Markdown code fences, while the Markdown file uses inline code formatting for file names and expected outputs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No subprocess, os.system, shell, eval, or similar execution API appears in the reviewed script. The backticks are formatting characters, not executable shell syntax.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Paths Controlled by CLI Arguments
dangerous_patterns.description: The script reads from a user-provided error file and writes debug outputs under a user-provided output directory. Operators should run it from a trusted workspace and avoid broad or sensitive paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly implemented through command-line arguments and filesystem operations. It is expected behavior for the tool, but it should be treated as a controlled filesystem capability.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parent Directory References in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions include relative paths that traverse outside the skill directory. This may lead an agent to consult unbundled or unreviewed files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The relative traversal paths are present in the reviewed Markdown. The risk is dependency and boundary confusion rather than confirmed data theft.

content.value_statement: Deep learning errors often invite broad, risky patches before the root cause is clear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through conservative diagnosis, evidence gathering, and minimal fix planning.
actual_capabilities: Classifies common deep learning failures from tracebacks or symptoms.
Suggests conservative next actions before repository code changes.
Separates debug fixes from research contributions and experiment changes.
Recommends savepoints before medium-risk or high-risk patch work.
Can write diagnosis, patch plan, and status reports through its helper script.
limitations: It does not automatically repair code without explicit user approval.
It focuses on concrete failures, not broad repository exploration.
It depends on the user providing useful error text or a traceback.
Its helper script performs local file reads and writes when invoked.

use_cases.title: Triage Training Failures
use_cases.description: Classify a traceback, CUDA error, NaN loss, or shape mismatch before changing model code.

use_cases.title: Review a Patch Plan
use_cases.description: Separate the likely root cause from the smallest safe fix and approval steps.

use_cases.title: Document Debug Evidence
use_cases.description: Create a concise diagnosis and action list for a failing experiment run.

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose a Traceback
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a pasted error.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use safe-debug on this traceback. Identify the likely category, the evidence, and the smallest safe next step before any patch.

prompt_templates.title: Check a CUDA OOM
prompt_templates.scenario: Memory failure during training or inference.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use safe-debug to analyze this CUDA out-of-memory failure. Prioritize configuration checks before model code changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Checkpoint Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: Checkpoint load errors or missing keys.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use safe-debug to review this checkpoint load failure. Explain whether the mismatch is expected and whether a savepoint is needed.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Proposed Debug Patch
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before changing research code.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use safe-debug to audit this proposed patch plan. Separate debug necessity from research impact and flag any experiment comparability risks.

output_examples.input: A training run fails with a tensor shape mismatch after changing the dataset head.
output_examples.output: The skill would identify a shape mismatch, list the failing boundary to inspect, recommend shape logging, and delay code changes until the cause is confirmed.

output_examples.input: A model fails to load a checkpoint because several keys are missing or unexpected.
output_examples.output: The skill would compare model variant assumptions, checkpoint source, and load strictness before suggesting any compatibility patch.

output_examples.input: A run starts producing NaN loss after a mixed-precision configuration change.
output_examples.output: The skill would check data ranges, loss inputs, learning rate, and precision settings before proposing architecture changes.

best_practices: Provide the full traceback, command, configuration, and recent change that preceded the failure.
Ask for diagnosis and evidence before requesting code edits.
Create a savepoint before approving changes that affect model logic or experiment meaning.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for broad refactoring when no concrete failure exists.
Do not ask it to patch code before the root cause is narrowed.
Do not treat a debug fix as a research contribution without checking comparability.

faq.question: What problems is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for concrete deep learning failures, including tracebacks, CUDA memory errors, checkpoint issues, shape mismatches, and NaN losses.

faq.question: Does it modify code automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs the assistant to diagnose first and require explicit approval before patching.

faq.question: Can it help with non-ML debugging?
faq.answer: It may help with general tracebacks, but its guidance is tuned for deep learning research workflows.

faq.question: What files can the helper script create?
faq.answer: When invoked, it can create diagnosis, patch plan, and status files in the selected output directory.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark medium risk?
faq.answer: The helper script uses local filesystem paths, and the instructions reference files outside the packaged skill directory.

faq.question: Is there evidence of malicious behavior?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The audit did not confirm shell execution, network access, credential access, or prompt injection.
